# UX Data Performance API Security Checklist: Storm Mode Forecast Adjustments

**Purpose**: Validate the quality of requirements across UX, data, performance, API, and security concerns for UC-15 before implementation work begins.
**Created**: 2026-03-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](/Users/sahmed/Documents/311-forecast-system/specs/015-uc-15-weather-storm-mode/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the operator-facing review needs defined beyond “storm mode happened,” including what details must be visible for validated activations, rejected triggers, weather-unavailable cases, baseline reversion, and notification follow-up outcomes? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3, Plan §API Contract Direction]
- [x] CHK002 Are requirements defined for how storm-mode evaluation aligns to both forecast-refresh and alert-evaluation timing so scope-limited activation occurs before dependent downstream logic for that same scope proceeds? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001 through §FR-007, Plan §Performance Goals, Data Model §StormModeEvaluationRun]
- [x] CHK003 Are the required attributes of a storm-mode activation fully specified, including scope boundaries, effective window, activation status, parameter profiles, and baseline-reversion semantics? [Completeness, Spec §Key Entities, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-016, Data Model §StormModeActivation]
- [x] CHK004 Are all intended API consumers covered, including operational managers and administrators who inspect current activations or detailed evaluations, rather than only the notification recipient persona? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §paths]
- [x] CHK005 Does the specification define what “monitor configured weather data feeds” means strongly enough to cover both scheduled and trigger-driven evaluation paths, supported weather-source types, and source-selection rules reused from UC-09? [Gap, Spec §FR-001, Plan §Summary, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is “more cautious forecasts and more sensitive alerts” translated into inspectable uncertainty and sensitivity parameter changes rather than left as an outcome-only statement? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-016]
- [x] CHK007 Is “affected category, geography, and time scope” unambiguous about canonical scope construction, optional geography behavior, and how time windows align with forecast products and alert evaluations? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-017, Data Model §Reused Shared Entities and Vocabulary, Data Model §StormModeTriggerAssessment]
- [x] CHK008 Is “validated storm condition that satisfies storm-mode criteria” precise enough to separate raw severe-weather detection from the approved business validation decision that is required before activation? [Clarity, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios User Story 1, Spec §FR-002, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK009 Is “continue with standard forecasting and alert logic” defined clearly enough to distinguish baseline behavior after weather unavailability, trigger rejection, and adjustment failure, especially because adjustment failure also forces alert sensitivity back to baseline? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-010 through §FR-013, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §Forecast Adjustment Status]
- [x] CHK010 Is “existing notification service workflow” specific enough about which alert paths UC-15 may reuse and how shared notification lineage is referenced from storm-mode records? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions, Data Model §Shared Notification Reference Type]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Are the scope-isolation requirements consistent between the user stories, edge cases, functional requirements, and activation data model so storm mode cannot leak into unaffected categories, geographies, or time windows? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-017, Data Model §StormModeActivation]
- [x] CHK012 Are fallback and failure-handling rules consistent between the spec’s wording and the plan’s stronger statement that forecast-adjustment failure forces both uncertainty and alert sensitivity to baseline for the same scope? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-013, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §StormModeAlertEvaluation]
- [x] CHK013 Are notification-delivery expectations consistent between the spec’s retry-eligible follow-up requirement and the plan or data-model choice to keep shared notification records as the canonical delivery source of truth? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Constraints, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §Overview]
- [x] CHK014 Are assumptions about reusing UC-09 weather conventions and UC-10 or UC-11 notification lineage consistent with the plan’s summary and data-model references, or do any required mapping rules remain implicit? [Consistency, Plan §Summary, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions, Data Model §Reused Shared Entities and Vocabulary]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can the success criteria objectively verify user-facing value for operational managers, or do they mostly confirm backend activation, widening, and logging without defining what reviewable diagnostic outputs must be usable? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Success Criteria, Spec §FR-016, Plan §API Contract Direction]
- [x] CHK016 Are performance goals measurable for every critical path, including activation lookup and detailed evaluation retrieval, rather than only requiring that downstream processing finish before dependent forecast or alert cycles continue? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001 through §SC-005, Plan §Performance Goals, Contract §GET /api/v1/storm-mode/activations/current, Contract §GET /api/v1/storm-mode/evaluations]
- [x] CHK017 Can the requirement to preserve traceability be objectively tested without a more explicit definition of required correlation identifiers, retention expectations, and minimum diagnostic fields for cross-record linkage? [Measurability, Spec §FR-015, Spec §SC-005, Data Model §StormModeEvaluationRun, Data Model §StormModeEvaluationView]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are the primary UX scenarios defined for both real-time operational awareness and later authenticated review of validated activations, rejected triggers, no-trigger passes, weather-unavailable outcomes, and baseline reversions? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories, Plan §API Contract Direction, Data Model §StormModeActivationView]
- [x] CHK019 Are alternate data scenarios specified for feeds that are reachable but stale, partially missing, or geographically mismatched, or is only total unavailability covered? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-001, Plan §Summary]
- [x] CHK020 Are recovery requirements defined for what should happen after a retry-eligible notification event is retried or manually reviewed, or is that downstream lifecycle intentionally out of scope once UC-15 preserves the linked notification state? [Coverage, Recovery Flow, Spec §FR-014, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK021 Are API error and authorization scenarios covered by written requirements for invalid scope filters, unauthorized diagnostic reads, and missing storm-mode evaluation identifiers? [Coverage, Gap, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §paths]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK022 Are boundary-case requirements defined for scopes where storm mode is active for forecast generation but alert sensitivity is intentionally unchanged because the scope is `not_applicable` for storm-adjusted alerting? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-006, Data Model §Alert Sensitivity Status]
- [x] CHK023 Are data and concurrency requirements defined for overlapping monitoring cycles, forecast refreshes, or manual replays that evaluate the same scope while activation state is changing? [Edge Case, Gap, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions, Data Model §StormModeEvaluationRun, Data Model §StormModeActivation]
- [x] CHK024 Are edge cases specified for activation-window expiry, overlapping validated triggers, and replacement of one active storm window by another for the same scope? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-003, Data Model §Activation Status, Data Model §StormModeActivation]
- [x] CHK025 Are API pagination, result-size, and filtering boundaries defined for evaluation-history review under larger operational histories, or do the current contracts leave list volume and query behavior implicit? [Edge Case, Gap, Contract §GET /api/v1/storm-mode/evaluations, Contract §GET /api/v1/storm-mode/activations/current]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK026 Are UX requirements defined for how baseline fallback, reverted-to-baseline status, and notification retry states should be communicated in diagnostics so operators can distinguish safe degradation from successful storm adjustment? [Non-Functional, UX, Gap, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3, Data Model §StormModeActivationView]
- [x] CHK027 Are data retention and auditability requirements specified for evaluation runs, trigger assessments, activation records, forecast-adjustment outcomes, alert evaluations, and linked notification references? [Non-Functional, Data, Gap, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-015, Plan §Storage, Data Model §Overview]
- [x] CHK028 Are performance requirements quantified for authenticated read paths and persistence of 100% terminal outcomes, not just for downstream selection before forecast-refresh and alert-evaluation cycles continue? [Non-Functional, Performance, Gap, Plan §Performance Goals, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK029 Are security requirements specific about role boundaries between operational managers, administrators, and any service actors permitted to trigger manual replay or inspect detailed storm-mode diagnostics? [Non-Functional, Security, Plan §Constitution Check, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §security]
- [x] CHK030 Are availability and operational-safety requirements defined for how diagnostic reads behave when weather providers or downstream notification systems are degraded but persisted storm-mode outcomes remain queryable? [Non-Functional, Reliability, Gap, Plan §Constitution Check, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK031 Are upstream dependencies on approved weather-provider conventions, retained forecast lineage, existing alert-evaluation flows, and shared notification infrastructure explicitly documented as requirement constraints rather than implementation-only assumptions? [Dependencies, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Constitution Check, Data Model §Overview]
- [x] CHK032 Is the assumption that the business will later define storm-validation criteria and alert-sensitivity scopes acceptable as written, or should the spec state a minimum contract for configuration provenance and versioning? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK033 Are external dependency requirements defined for weather-source timeout handling, stale-data detection, source attribution, and failure-classification vocabulary at the trigger-assessment level? [Dependency, Gap, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-010, Data Model §StormModeTriggerAssessment]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK034 Is there any conflict between “activate storm mode only for the affected scope” and the plan’s weather-source reuse rules if provider coverage or geography matching imply different scope granularities? [Conflict, Spec §FR-003, Plan §Summary, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions]
- [x] CHK035 Is “one correlated operational record trail” established clearly enough in the requirements, or does it currently depend more on plan and data-model decisions than on explicit spec language? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-015, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions, Data Model §StormModeEvaluationRun]
- [x] CHK036 Are the terms “storm mode trigger,” “activation,” “forecast adjustment,” “alert sensitivity,” “baseline applied,” and “reverted to baseline” used consistently enough across spec, plan, data model, and API contract to support one canonical implementation vocabulary? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Data Model Direction, Data Model §Canonical UC-15 Vocabulary, Contract §components.schemas]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline.
- This checklist is intentionally lightweight and author-oriented, with equal coverage across UX, data, performance, API, and security requirement quality.
